purpose: Hardened JSON I/O and schema validation utilities. `readJSON(path)` and `readJSONAsync(path)` swallow all errors and return `null` on failure — but log SyntaxError specifically to console.error (so malformed JSON is visible while ENOENT is silent). `readJSONWithRetry(path, delayMs)` is the workhorse for progress files: it reads once, and if the result is null (likely a mid-write empty/partial file from a worker), it waits `PROGRESS_RETRY_MS` (80ms default) and reads again — this races against the worker's atomic rename. `writeAtomic`/`writeAtomicAsync` implement write-rename atomicity by writing to `<path>.tmp` and renaming to the final path; on error the .tmp file is cleaned up best-effort and the error is rethrown. Schema validators `isValidInitialization`, `isValidProgress(data, expectedTaskId, expectedDashboardId)`, and `isValidLogs` enforce the canonical document shapes: initialization requires `task` (null or object with name/type), `agents` array (each with id+title), and validates `task.type` is in `['Waves','Chains']`; progress requires task_id+status, status in `['in_progress','completed','failed']`, optional stage in `['reading_context','planning','implementing','testing','finalizing','completed','failed']`, and rejects mismatched task_id/dashboard_id (a cross-worker write violation guard); logs requires `entries` array.
gotchas: `readJSON` returns null on BOTH 'file does not exist' AND 'malformed JSON' — callers cannot distinguish without checking fs.existsSync first. Only SyntaxError is logged.
`readJSONWithRetry` only retries ONCE — if a worker is doing a slow non-atomic write, the second read may also fail and return null silently. Workers SHOULD use writeAtomic to avoid this.
`writeAtomic` rename is atomic ONLY on the same filesystem — across mounts it falls back to copy+delete, breaking atomicity. All Synapse paths are under ROOT so this is safe by convention.

`isValidProgress` REJECTS data when `expectedTaskId` is provided and doesn't match — and logs a CROSS-WORKER WRITE VIOLATION error. This is intentional security: the validate-progress-file.sh hook depends on this rejection.
`isValidProgress` allows `completed_at` to be absent OR null on in_progress — but rejects it being a non-null string. A worker that sets completed_at while still in_progress will fail validation.
`isValidInitialization` enforces `task.type in ['Waves','Chains']` — case-sensitive, capitalized. Any other value (e.g. 'waves' lowercase) fails validation.
Stage must be one of seven lowercase values — adding a new stage requires updating this validator OR validation will reject worker progress.
patterns: Read-fail-silently pattern: `try {return JSON.parse(readFileSync(p))} catch {return null}` — callers branch on null.
Mid-write resilience: `readJSONWithRetry` waits 80ms and retries once before giving up — designed for fs.watch races against worker writes.
Atomic write: write to `<path>.tmp`, rename to `<path>`. Best-effort cleanup of .tmp on failure.
Schema validation returns boolean — callers log/skip on false, never throw.
Cross-worker write detection: `expectedTaskId` mismatch in progress data returns false AND logs to stderr — this is a hook-enforced security boundary.
conventions: All readers return null on any failure (never throw).
All writers throw on failure (never silent).
Schema validators are pure functions named `isValid<Shape>`.

depended_by: src/server/services/* (DashboardService, WatcherService, QueueService, ArchiveService, HistoryService, DependencyService)
